questions.scenario: `orders-db-url` exists in Secret Manager. Version 3 contains the current database connection string. Which part is usually the dangerous payload?

options.text: The public fact that a secret named `orders-db-url` exists.

questions.explanation: The secret name is the stable container. The version stores the sensitive value.

questions.scenario: The team wants to rotate the database URL without changing every reference in app code. Which Secret Manager feature helps?

options.text: Add a new secret version under the same secret name.
Paste the new URL into a ticket.
Rename the production project for every rotation.
Grant Owner to the runtime service account.

questions.explanation: Versions let the secret name remain stable while the stored value changes.

questions.scenario: Which statements about secrets and versions are correct?

options.text: A secret is the named container.
A secret version stores a value.
Old versions can be disabled or destroyed as part of rotation.
Every engineer should read the payload to prove the secret exists.

questions.explanation: Secret review should use metadata and access evidence, not broad payload exposure.
